learning_outcomes.description: Analyse industrial chemical and manufacturing processes to identify and quantify major sources of greenhouse gas emissions across material, energy, and utility systems.
Develop emissions inventories and apply life cycle assessment (LCA) methods to calculate emissions intensities and related environmental performance metrics for industrial processes.
Propose technically feasible decarbonisation strategies for existing industrial plants, drawing on process modifications, technology substitution, electrification, and carbon mitigation options.
Evaluate and compare decarbonisation options by estimating changes in process performance, lifecycle emissions, and capital and operating costs to support evidence based engineering decisions.

assessments.weighting: <dd>20% each problem set is worth 4%. We take the best 5 of 8 possible submissions.  </dd>

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete a problem set on a particular course topic. There are a maximum of 8 possible problem sets.

For each student we will identify the 5 submissions with the highest mark which will then be aggregated (for a total mark out of 15%).

Problem sets published on Blackboard. Students will submit individually.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The complete project description will be posted to Blackboard in Week 1.

The project will focus on the decarbonisation pathways for an industrial facility in Australia. Information about specific facilities in Australia will be provided, although students may propose an alternate facility with justification. Ultimately the decision of whether the alternate facility is permitted will be at the sole discretion of the Course Coordinator.

The project will be completed individually. The interim report will focus on establishing the baseline performance and life cycle analysis of the facility.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Reports should be submitted through Blackboard. Students are welcome to submit their calculation and analysis files if desired.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The complete project description will be posted to Blackboard in Week 1.

The project will focus on the decarbonisation pathways for an industrial facility in Australia. Information about specific facilities in Australia will be provided, although students may propose an alternate facility with justification. Ultimately the decision of whether the alternate facility is permitted will be at the sole discretion of the Course Coordinator.

The project will be completed individually. In the final report, students will be required to evaluate different decarbonisation options, and propose a pathway forward for that facility to comply with Australia's Safeguard mechanism and Net Zero by 2050 target.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam will assess all topics covered in the course including through lectures, tutorials and other assessment items.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures and guest lectures

Core lectures delivered by the teaching team will focus on Australian industries, decarbonisation pathways, and tools that help evaluate the various decarbonisation options. These will include industries like alumina, aluminium, cement, fertilisers, fuels, iron / steel, plastics. Decarbonisation pathways will typically follow a hierachy of offsets, fuel switching, electrification, carbon capture and storage, and new process chemistries. Tools will build on existing knowledge but may include life cycle assessment, economic analysis and multi-criteria decision making.

Guest lecturers may deliver lectures related to specific industries or pathways depending on availability.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Workshops and tutorials

Tutorials will be focussed on building experience and expertise with the tools, industries and decarbonisation pathways. The problem sets will be the focus of the tutorials and will build skills for both the project and the exam.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
